title: A Hate-Song.
content:
A hater he came and sat by a ditch,
    And he took an old cracked lute;
    And he sang a song which was more of a screech
    'Gainst a woman that was a brute.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Percy Bysshe Shelley, titled "A Hate-Song.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
